questions.question_text: Carbohydrates Which taste sweet are called
questions.question_html: Carbohydrates Which taste sweet are called
questions.explanation: Carbohydrates like monosaccharides (one sugar molecule, example: Glucose) and disaccharides (two sugar molecule,example Sucrose I.e Table sugar) always tastes sweet.

questions.question_text: The following molecule is called as
questions.question_html: The following molecule is called as
questions.explanation: It is aldohexose. An aldohexose is a hexose with an aldehyde group on one end.it is naturally occurring in nature and is found in fruits.

questions.question_text: The following compound can be called as
questions.question_html: The following compound can be called as
questions.explanation: Glucose is correctly named as D(+)-glucose. ‘D’ before the name of glucose represents the configuration whereas ‘(+)’ represents dextrorotatory nature of the molecule. When the -OH group lies on right hand side in the structure it is said to have D configuration. In (+) glucose, -OH on the lowest asymmetric carbon is on the right side so (+) glucose is assigned D-configuration.

questions.question_text: The spatial arrangement of the given molecule is denoted by
questions.question_html: The spatial arrangement of the given molecule is denoted by

options.text: D or L sign before the molecule.
options.html: D or L sign before the molecule.

questions.correct_option_text: D or L sign before the molecule.
questions.explanation: The letters ‘D’ or ‘L’ before the name of any compound indicate the relative configuration of a particular stereoisomer of a compound with respect to configuration of some other compound, configuration of which is known. In the case of carbohydrates, this refers to their relation with a particular isomer of glyceraldehyde. When the -OH group lies on right hand side in the structure it is said to have D configuration. In L configuration -OH group is on left hand side of the asymmetric C.

questions.question_text: Choose the appropriate answer(s) for the below representation from the options given
questions.question_html: Choose the appropriate answer(s) for the below representation from the options given
questions.explanation: Glyceraldehyde has one chiral centre (the asymmetric C) and has two enantiomers as we can see they are mirror images of each other. Stereoisomers of a compound are those isomers of the compound which have same molecular formula but different spatial arrangements of their atoms in space. So these compounds are stereoisomers. These compounds are dextrorotatory (+) isomer and laevorotatory (-) isomers as one rotates the path of light in right direction whereas the other rotates in left direction.

questions.question_text: The number of asymmetric carbon atom(s) in below figure is are
questions.question_html: The number of asymmetric carbon atom(s) in below figure is are
questions.explanation: Here there is only 1 asymmetrical carbon as to 1 C four different groups are attached i.e. -CH 2 OH, -OH, -H, -CHO.

questions.question_text: What is the most abundant organic compound on earth?
questions.question_html: What is the most abundant organic compound on earth?
questions.explanation: Glucose is probably the most abundant organic compound on earth. It is the monomer of many of the larger carbohydrates, namely starch, cellulose

questions.question_text: The polymer(s) of dextrose is
questions.question_html: The polymer(s) of dextrose is
questions.explanation: Monosaccharides (such as Glucose/dextrose) are building blocks of disaccharides (such as sucrose and lactose) and polysaccharides (such as Cellulose and Starch).

questions.question_text: Oxime is formed by treating glucose with
questions.question_html: Oxime is formed by treating glucose with
questions.explanation: Glucose reacts with hydroxylamine (NH 2 OH) to form an oxime. Oxime is formed by combination of carbonyl group of glucose and hydroxyl group of hydroxyl amine.

questions.question_text: Acetylation of glucose yields
questions.question_html: Acetylation of glucose yields
questions.explanation: Acetylation of glucose with acetic anhydride gives glucose pentaacetate which confirms the presence of five –OH groups. Since it exists as a stable compound, five –OH groups should be attached to different carbon atoms.

questions.question_text: Hydrolysis of starch yields
questions.question_html: Hydrolysis of starch yields
questions.explanation: Glucose is the monomer of many of the larger carbohydrates, namely starch, cellulose. Hydrolysis of starch gives glucose.

questions.question_text: Glucose does not give Schiff’s test because of the formation of cyclic ________.
questions.question_html: Glucose does not give Schiff’s test because of the formation of cyclic ________.
questions.explanation: Schiff's test is given by aldehydes. Despite having the aldehyde group, glucose does not give Schiff’s test as aldehydic group is not free here. It was proposed that one of the —OH groups may add to the —CHO group and form a cyclic hemiacetal structure. It was found that glucose forms a six-membered ring in which —OH at C-5 is involved in ring formation. This explains the absence of —CHO group and also existence of glucose in two cyclic hemiacetal forms α-D-(+)-Glucose and β-D-(+)-Glucose.

questions.question_text: The cyclic form of fructose is called
questions.question_html: The cyclic form of fructose is called
questions.explanation: Cyclic form of fructose is called Fructofuranose. The ring, thus formed is a five membered ring and is named as furanose with analogy to the compound furan. Furan is a five membered cyclic compound with one oxygen and four carbon atoms.

questions.question_text: Glycosidic linkage is found between
questions.question_html: Glycosidic linkage is found between
questions.explanation: Glycosidic bond is an ether linkage between two monosaccharides.

questions.question_text: Honey contains primarily
questions.question_html: Honey contains primarily
questions.explanation: Honey is a high carbohydrate substance. It also contains proteins.

questions.question_text: Glycogen is formed in
questions.question_html: Glycogen is formed in
questions.explanation: Carbohydrates are used as storage molecules as starch in plants and glycogen in animals. Glycogen is called animal starch because its structure is similar to that of amylopectin and it is rather more branched. It is present in liver, muscles and brain. When the body needs energy, it breaks down the available glycogen and converts it to glucose which can be further utilised to give energy. Glycogen is also present in fungi and yeast.

questions.question_text: D – ribose and 2 – deoxy – D – ribose are
questions.question_html: D – ribose and 2 – deoxy – D – ribose are
questions.explanation: Two aldopentoses viz. D-ribose and 2-deoxy-D-ribose are present in nucleic acids.

questions.question_text: Amino acids are components of
questions.question_html: Amino acids are components of
questions.explanation: Proteins are formed by combination of α-amino acids.

questions.question_text: In which one of the following options, both compounds have a sweet taste?
questions.question_html: In which one of the following options, both compounds have a sweet taste?
questions.explanation: Both glucose and glycine have sweet taste.

questions.question_text: Protein is generally a polypeptide with _______ amino acid residues.
questions.question_html: Protein is generally a polypeptide with _______ amino acid residues.
questions.explanation: A polypeptide with more than hundred amino acid residues, having molecular mass higher than 10,000 u is called a protein. However, the distinction between a polypeptide and a protein is not very sharp. Polypeptides with fewer amino acids are likely to be called proteins if they ordinarily have a well defined conformation of a protein such as insulin which contains 51 amino acids.

questions.question_text: Insulin is a protein which contains _______ amino acids
questions.question_html: Insulin is a protein which contains _______ amino acids
questions.explanation: Insulin contain 51 amino acids

questions.question_text: The bond –CO–NH– is called
questions.question_html: The bond –CO–NH– is called
questions.explanation: Proteins are the polymers of α-amino acids and they are connected to each other by peptide bond or peptide linkage. Chemically, peptide linkage is an amide formed between –COOH group and –NH 2 group. The reaction between two molecules of similar or different amino acids, proceeds through the combination of the amino group of one molecule with the carboxyl group of the other. This results in the elimination of a water molecule and formation of a peptide bond –CO–NH–

questions.question_text: In aqueous solution, amino acids behave like:
questions.question_html: In aqueous solution, amino acids behave like:
questions.explanation: Amino acids are water-soluble, high melting solids and behave like salts rather than simple amines or carboxylic acids. This behaviour is due to the presence of both acidic (carboxyl group) and basic (amino group) groups in the same molecule. In aqueous solution, the carboxyl group can lose a proton and amino group can accept a proton, giving rise to a dipolar ion known as zwitter ion. This is neutral but contains both positive and negative charges.

questions.question_text: Enzymes are used in the various chemical reactions in living organisms as they are
questions.question_html: Enzymes are used in the various chemical reactions in living organisms as they are
questions.explanation: Enzymes are bio-catalysts. Almost all the enzymes are globular proteins. Enzymes are very specific for a particular reaction and for a particular substrate. Enzymes are needed only in small quantities for the progress of a reaction. Similar to the action of chemical catalysts, enzymes are said to reduce the magnitude of activation energy for biochemical reactions.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is most soluble in water?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is most soluble in water?
questions.explanation: Amino acids are water-soluble, high melting solids and behave like salts rather than simple amines or carboxylic acids. This behaviour is due to the presence of both acidic (carboxyl group) and basic (amino group) groups in the same molecule. In aqueous solution, the carboxyl group can lose a proton and amino group can accept a proton, giving rise to a dipolar ion known as zwitter ion. This is neutral but contains both positive and negative charges. Because of the resulting polar interactions in water they are soluble in water.

questions.question_text: Zwitter ionic form of amino acids behave like
questions.question_html: Zwitter ionic form of amino acids behave like
questions.explanation: Amino acids behave like salts rather than simple amines or carboxylic acids. This behaviour is due to the presence of both acidic (carboxyl group) and basic (amino group) groups in the same molecule. In aqueous solution, the carboxyl group can lose a proton and amino group can accept a proton, giving rise to a dipolar ion known as zwitter ion. This is neutral but contains both positive and negative charges.

questions.question_text: Salivary amylase is secreted by
questions.question_html: Salivary amylase is secreted by
questions.explanation: Salivary amylase is an enzyme secreted by salivary glands.

questions.question_text: Reducing sugars are
questions.question_html: Reducing sugars are

options.text: those carbohydrates which reduce Tollen's reagent
options.html: those carbohydrates which reduce Tollen's reagent

options.text: those carbohydrates which reduce Fehling’s solution
options.html: those carbohydrates which reduce Fehling’s solution

questions.explanation: All those carbohydrates which reduce Fehling’s solution and Tollens’ reagent are referred to as reducing sugars. All monosaccharides whether aldose or ketose are reducing sugars.

questions.question_text: Commercially glucose is obtained by
questions.question_html: Commercially glucose is obtained by

options.text: hydrolysis of starch by boiling it with dilute H 2 SO 4 at 393 K under pressure.
options.html: hydrolysis of starch by boiling it with dilute H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub> at 393 K under pressure.

options.text: boiling sucrose with dilute HCl or H 2 SO 4 in alcoholic solution
options.html: boiling sucrose with dilute HCl or H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub> in alcoholic solution

questions.correct_option_text: hydrolysis of starch by boiling it with dilute H 2 SO 4 at 393 K under pressure.
questions.explanation: Commercially glucose is obtained by hydrolysis of starch by boiling it with dilute H 2 SO 4 at 393 K under pressure (2-3 atm). {tex}(C_6H_{10}O_5)_n + nH_2O \rightarrow nC_6H_{12}O_6{/tex}

questions.question_text: The biological polymer is
questions.question_html: The biological polymer is
questions.explanation: The particles in nucleus of the cell, responsible for heredity, are called chromosomes which are made up of proteins and another type of biomolecules called nucleic acids. These are mainly of two types, the deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) and ribonucleic acid (RNA). Since nucleic acids are long chain polymers of nucleotides, so they are also called polynucleotides. Thus nucleic acid is a biological polymer.

questions.question_text: The following structure of protein is called
questions.question_html: The following structure of protein is called
questions.explanation: This structure represents tertiary structure of proteins. The tertiary structure of proteins represents overall folding of the polypeptide chains i.e., further folding of the secondary structure.

questions.question_text: The sugar constituent of DNA is
questions.question_html: The sugar constituent of DNA is
questions.explanation: Complete hydrolysis of DNA yields a pentose sugar, phosphoric acid and nitrogen containing heterocyclic compounds (called bases). In DNA molecules, the sugar moiety is β-D-2-deoxyribose.

questions.question_text: Water soluble vitamins must be supplied regularly in diet because
questions.question_html: Water soluble vitamins must be supplied regularly in diet because

options.text: they get used up very fast in body.
options.html: they get used up very fast in body.

options.text: they cannot be provided by synthetic means.
options.html: they cannot be provided by synthetic means.

options.text: they are readily excreted in urine and cannot be stored (except vitamin B 12 ) in our body.
options.html: they are readily excreted in urine and cannot be stored (except vitamin B<sub>12</sub>) in our body.

questions.correct_option_text: they are readily excreted in urine and cannot be stored (except vitamin B 12 ) in our body.
questions.explanation: B group vitamins and vitamin C are soluble in water so they are grouped together as water soluble vitamins. These must be supplied regularly in diet because they are readily excreted in urine and cannot be stored (except vitamin B 12 ) in our body.

questions.question_text: ___ are considered as essential food factors.
questions.question_html: ___  are considered as essential food factors.
questions.explanation: Certain organic compounds are required in small amounts in our diet but their deficiency causes specific diseases. These compounds are called vitamins. Most of the vitamins cannot be synthesised in our body but plants can synthesise almost all of them, so they are considered as essential food factors. However, the bacteria of the gut can produce some of the vitamins required by us. All the vitamins are generally available in our diet.

questions.question_text: Adenosine is
questions.question_html: Adenosine is
questions.explanation: A unit formed by the attachment of a base to 1′ position of sugar is known as nucleoside. Adenosine is nucleoside composed of adenine attached a ribose sugar molecule.

questions.question_text: ATP is
questions.question_html: ATP is
questions.explanation: When nucleoside is linked to phosphoric acid at 5′-position of sugar moiety, we get a nucleotide. ATP (Adenosine triphosphate) is nucleotide of adenosine with 3 phosphoric acid molecules.

questions.question_text: RNA guides the biosynthesis of
questions.question_html: RNA guides the biosynthesis of
questions.explanation: Various RNA (ribonucleic acid) molecules in the cell guide synthesise the proteins.

questions.question_text: Another name of Vitamin B 2 is
questions.question_html: Another name of  Vitamin B2 is
questions.explanation: Vitamin B 2 is also called Riboflavin. It deficiency cause Cheilosis.

questions.question_text: Deficiency of Vitamin B 12 causes
questions.question_html: Deficiency of Vitamin B12 causes
questions.explanation: Deficiency of Vitamin B 12 results in Pernicious anaemia (Red Blood Cells deficient in haemoglobin)

questions.question_text: Nucleotides are joined together by
questions.question_html: Nucleotides are joined together by
questions.explanation: Nucleotides are together by phosphodiester linkage between 5′ and 3′ carbon atoms of the pentose sugar.

questions.question_text: Two nucleic acid chains are wound about each other and held together by hydrogen bonds between pairs of bases, this is called
questions.question_html: Two nucleic acid chains are wound about each other and held together by hydrogen bonds between pairs of bases, this is called
questions.explanation: DNA has a double strand helix structure. Two nucleic acid chains are wound about each other and held together by hydrogen bonds between pairs of bases. The two strands are complementary to each other because the hydrogen bonds are formed between specific pairs of bases. In secondary structure of RNA, helices are present which are only single stranded. Sometimes they fold back on themselves to form a double helix structure.

questions.question_text: Which one is not the essential amino acid in the ones given below?
questions.question_html: Which one is not the essential amino acid in the ones given below?
questions.explanation: Those amino acids which cannot be synthesised in the body and must be obtained through diet, are known as essential amino acids. Proline is not an essential amino acid.

questions.question_text: The carbohydrate which cannot be hydrolysed in human digestive system is
questions.question_html: The carbohydrate which cannot be hydrolysed in human digestive system is
questions.explanation: Cellulose occurs exclusively in plants and it is the most abundant organic substance in plant kingdom. It is a predominant constituent of cell wall of plant cells. Cellulose cannot be hydrolysed in human digestive system because the enzymes required to digest it are not present in human body.

questions.question_text: Cheilosis is caused by deficiency of
questions.question_html: Cheilosis is caused by deficiency of
questions.explanation: Deficiency of Vitamin B 2 (Riboflavin) results in Cheilosis (fissuring at corners of mouth and lips).

questions.question_text: The carrier of hereditary character is.
questions.question_html: The carrier of hereditary character is.
questions.explanation: Information regarding the sequence of nucleotides in the chain of a nucleic acid is called its primary structure. This gives information about sequence of bases in DNA which is primarily responsible for the hereditary character. Thus nucleotides are carrier of hereditary character.

questions.question_text: Building unit of a protein is
questions.question_html: Building unit of a protein is
questions.explanation: Proteins are the polymers of α-amino acids. So building unit of a protein is α – Aminoacid.

questions.question_text: Protein is a
questions.question_html: Protein is a
questions.explanation: In proteins α-amino acids are connected to each other by peptide bond or peptide linkage. Chemically, peptide linkage is an amide formed between –COOH group and –NH 2 group. The reaction between two molecules of similar or different amino acids, proceeds through the combination of the amino group of one molecule with the carboxyl group of the other. This results in the elimination of a water molecule and formation of a peptide bond –CO–NH–. So protein is condensation polymer.

questions.question_text: Addison’s disease is characterised by
questions.question_html: Addison’s disease is characterised by
questions.explanation: If adrenal cortex does not function properly then one of the results may be Addison’s disease characterised by hypoglycemia, weakness and increased susceptibility to stress.

questions.question_text: Progesterone is responsible for
questions.question_html: Progesterone is responsible for

options.text: preparing the uterus for implantation of fertilised egg.
options.html: preparing the uterus for implantation of fertilised egg.

questions.correct_option_text: preparing the uterus for implantation of fertilised egg.
questions.explanation: The combination of ovum and sperm forms the fertilized egg which further gets implanted in the uterus. This is brought about by the hormone progesterone which prepares uterus for this process.

questions.question_text: The two hormones which together regulate the glucose level in the blood
questions.question_html: The two hormones which together regulate the glucose level in the blood
questions.explanation: The role of insulin is to keep the blood glucose level within the narrow limit. Insulin is released in response to the rapid rise in blood glucose level. On the other hand hormone glucagon tends to increase the glucose level in the blood. The two hormones together regulate the glucose level in the blood.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a fibrous protein?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is a fibrous protein?
questions.explanation: When the polypeptide chains run parallel and are held together by hydrogen and disulphide bonds, then fibre– like structure is formed. Such proteins called fibrous proteins are generally insoluble in water. A common example is keratin (present in hair, wool, silk).

questions.question_text: DNA fingerprinting is used
questions.question_html: DNA fingerprinting is used

options.text: to determine paternity of an individual
options.html: to determine paternity of an individual

questions.explanation: DNA fingerprinting is now used in forensic laboratories for identification of criminals. to determine paternity of an individual. to identify the dead bodies in any accident by comparing the DNA’s of parents or children. to identify racial groups to rewrite biological evolution.

questions.question_text: An example of globular protein is
questions.question_html: An example of globular protein is
questions.explanation: Globular proteins are formed when the chains of polypeptides coil around to give a spherical shape. These are usually soluble in water. For example albumin and insulin.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following is not a globular protein?
questions.question_html: Which one of the following is not a globular protein?
questions.explanation: Globular proteins are formed when the chains of polypeptides coil around to give a spherical shape. These are usually soluble in water. When the polypeptide chains run parallel and are held together by hydrogen and disulphide bonds, then fibre– like structure is formed. Such proteins called fibrous proteins are generally insoluble in water. Myosin (present in muscles), is a fibrous protein and not a globular protein.

questions.question_text: Amino acid is.
questions.question_html: Amino acid is.
questions.explanation: Amino acids contain amino (–NH 2 ) and carboxyl (–COOH) functional groups.

questions.question_text: {tex}\mathop {{C_{12}}{H_{22}}{O_{11}}}\limits_{Maltose} \to \mathop {2{C_6}{H_{12}}{O_6}}\limits_{Glu\cos e} {/tex} In the above reaction, the enzyme that catalyses hydrolysis of maltose into glucose is
questions.question_html:
{tex}\mathop {{C_{12}}{H_{22}}{O_{11}}}\limits_{Maltose} \to \mathop {2{C_6}{H_{12}}{O_6}}\limits_{Glu\cos e} {/tex}
In the above reaction, the enzyme that catalyses hydrolysis of maltose into glucose is
questions.explanation: The enzyme which is responsible for catalysing the hydrolysis of maltose to glucose in the given reaction is named as maltase.

questions.question_text: The vitamin that cannot be stored in our body is
questions.question_html: The vitamin that cannot be stored in our body is
questions.explanation: B group vitamins and vitamin C are soluble in water so they are grouped together as water soluble vitamins, these must be supplied regularly in diet because they are readily excreted in urine and cannot be stored (except vitamin B 12 ) in our body.

questions.question_text: Certain vitamins cannot be stored in the body because
questions.question_html: Certain vitamins cannot be stored in the body because

options.text: they are readily excreted in urine
options.html: they are readily excreted in urine

options.text: they are used up very fast in the body
options.html: they are used up very fast in the body

questions.correct_option_text: they are readily excreted in urine
questions.explanation: B group vitamins and vitamin C are soluble in water so they are grouped together as water soluble vitamins. These must be supplied regularly in diet because they are readily excreted in urine and cannot be stored (except vitamin B 12 ) in our body.

questions.question_text: One or more of the following vitamin is insoluble in water
questions.question_html: One or more of the following vitamin is insoluble in water
questions.explanation: Vitamins which are soluble in fat and oils but insoluble in water are kept in fat soluble vitamins. These are vitamins A, D, E and K. They are stored in liver and adipose (fat storing) tissues. All these are insoluble in water.

questions.question_text: The vitamins are generally stored in the body in the
questions.question_html: The vitamins are generally stored in the body in the
questions.explanation: Vitamins which are soluble in fat and oils but insoluble in water are kept in fat soluble vitamins. These are vitamins A, D, E and K. These are generally stored in liver and adipose (fat storing) tissues.

questions.question_text: Night blindness is caused by the deficiency of
questions.question_html: Night blindness is caused by the deficiency of
questions.explanation: Vitamin A keeps eyes in good health. Deficiency of Vitamin A causes Night Blindness.

questions.question_text: Complete hydrolysis of RNA yields
questions.question_html: Complete hydrolysis of RNA yields
questions.explanation: Complete hydrolysis of RNA yields a pentose sugar, phosphoric acid and nitrogen containing heterocyclic compounds (called bases). In RNA molecule, the sugar moiety is β-D-ribose.

questions.question_text: DNA and RNA contain four bases each. Which of the following bases is not present in RNA?
questions.question_html: DNA and RNA contain four bases each. Which of the following bases is not present in RNA?
questions.explanation: DNA contains four bases viz. adenine (A), guanine (G), cytosine (C) and thymine (T). RNA also contains four bases, the first three bases are same as in DNA but the fourth one is uracil (U).

questions.question_text: The combination of nitrogen – containing heterocyclic base with 1ꞌ position of sugar is known as
questions.question_html: The combination of nitrogen – containing heterocyclic base with 1ꞌ position of sugar is known as
questions.explanation: A unit formed by the attachment of a base to 1′ position of sugar is known as nucleoside. In nucleosides, the sugar carbons are numbered as 1′, 2′, 3′, etc. in order to distinguish these from the bases.

questions.question_text: When a nucleoside is linked to phosphoric acid which of the following may be obtained?
questions.question_html: When a nucleoside is linked to phosphoric acid which of the following may be obtained?
questions.explanation: When nucleoside is linked to phosphoric acid at 5ꞌ – position of sugar moiety, we get a nucleotide.

questions.question_text: ____ are joined together by phosphodiester linkage between 5′ and 3′ carbon atoms of the pentose sugar.
questions.question_html: ____ are joined together by phosphodiester linkage between 5′ and 3′ carbon atoms of the pentose sugar.
questions.explanation: When nucleoside is linked to phosphoric acid at 5′-position of sugar moiety, we get a nucleotide and nucleotides are joined by a phosphodiester bond between 5′ and 3′ carbon atoms of the pentose sugar.

questions.question_text: The most appropriate base pair in a double helix of DNA is
questions.question_html: The most appropriate base pair in a double helix of DNA is
questions.explanation: Two nucleic acid chains are wound about each other and held together by hydrogen bonds between pairs of bases. The two strands are complementary to each other because the hydrogen bonds are formed between specific pairs of bases. Adenine forms hydrogen bonds with thymine whereas cytosine forms hydrogen bonds with guanine. So base pair in a double helix of DNA is A-T.

questions.question_text: DNA fingerprinting means the
questions.question_html: DNA fingerprinting means the

options.text: sequencing the bases present in double helix
options.html: sequencing the bases present in double helix

options.text: information regarding the unique sequence of bases on DNA for a person
options.html: information regarding the unique sequence of bases on DNA for a person

options.text: information reagrding the unique imprints on the fingertip for a person
options.html: information reagrding the unique imprints on the fingertip for a person

questions.correct_option_text: information regarding the unique sequence of bases on DNA for a person
questions.explanation: A sequence of bases on DNA is unique for a person and information regarding this is called DNA fingerprinting. It is same for every cell and cannot be altered by any known treatment.

questions.question_text: The most appropriate structure for knowing about the sequence of nucleotides in the DNA chain is
questions.question_html: The most appropriate structure for knowing about the sequence of nucleotides in the DNA chain is
questions.explanation: Proteins may have one or more polypeptide chains. Each polypeptide in a protein has amino acids linked with each other in a specific sequence and it is this sequence of amino acids that is said to be the primary structure of that protein. Thus the most appropriate structure for knowing about the sequence of nucleotides in the DNA chain is its primary structure.

questions.question_text: Curdling of milk which is caused due to
questions.question_html: Curdling of milk which is caused due to

options.text: formation of lactic acid by the bacteria present in milk and resulting denaturation
options.html: formation of lactic acid by the bacteria present in milk and resulting denaturation

options.text: disturbance in primary structure of milk proteins
options.html: disturbance in primary structure of milk proteins

questions.correct_option_text: formation of lactic acid by the bacteria present in milk and resulting denaturation
questions.explanation: Curdling of milk is caused due to the formation of lactic acid by the bacteria present in milk. Milk contains proteins. When a protein in its native form, is subjected to physical change like change in temperature or chemical change like change in pH, the hydrogen bonds are disturbed. Due to this, globules unfold and helix get uncoiled and protein loses its biological activity. This is called denaturation of protein.

questions.question_text: The major component of starch is:
questions.question_html: The major component of starch is:
questions.explanation: Starch is a polymer of α-glucose and consists of two components - Amylose and Amylopectin. Amylose is water-soluble component which constitutes about 15-20% of starch. Amylopectin is insoluble in water and constitutes about 80-85% of starch. Thus, Amylopectin is the major component of starch.

options.html: those carbohydrates which reduce Tollen's reagent

options.text: those carbohydrates which reduce Fehling’s solution
options.html: those carbohydrates which reduce Fehling’s solution

questions.explanation: All those carbohydrates which reduce Fehling’s solution and Tollens’ reagent are referred to as reducing sugars. All monosaccharides whether aldose or ketose are reducing sugars.

questions.question_html: Acetylation of glucose yields
questions.explanation: Acetylation of glucose with acetic anhydride gives glucose pentaacetate which confirms the presence of five –OH groups. Since it exists as a stable compound, five –OH groups should be attached to different carbon atoms.

questions.question_html: The following structure of protein is called
questions.explanation: This structure represents tertiary structure of proteins. The tertiary structure of proteins represents overall folding of the polypeptide chains i.e., further folding of the secondary structure.

questions.question_text: RNA guides the biosynthesis of
questions.question_html: RNA guides the biosynthesis of
questions.explanation: Various RNA (ribonucleic acid) molecules in the cell guide synthesise the proteins.
